title: phpMyAdmin Upload Limit Too Small? Here’s What Actually Works
description: Stuck with a 2MB or 50MB upload cap? Use these practical options without server access.
author: SQLSplit

content.text: Many hosts cap phpMyAdmin uploads at 2–50MB. If your dump is bigger, uploads fail before import even starts.
Split your SQL file into smaller chunks that fit the limit. Import the first chunk (schema) and then the rest.

items: Use MySQL CLI if you have SSH
Ask host support to import it

content.text: See also: **How to Import Large SQL Files in cPanel**.

content.title: Bypass upload limits
content.text: Split your file into upload‑friendly chunks.

faq.question: Can I raise the upload limit myself?
faq.answer: Usually no, unless you control the server settings.

faq.question: What chunk size should I use?
faq.answer: Use 10–20MB for phpMyAdmin on shared hosting.
